channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kasipul MP Aspirant Advocates for Monthly SHA Payments to Support Vulnerable Kenyans
item.description: Homa Bay’s Kasipul parliamentary aspirant, Newton Ogada, has raised concerns over the Social Health Authority (SHA) payments, urging the government to make them affordable and equitable. Speaking to T...

item.title: Midgitte Bardot: Challenging Prejudice and Empowering Through Drag
item.description: Tamm Reynolds, a non-binary trans performer with dwarfism, has gained recognition for their alter ego, Midgitte Bardot, through daring and provocative performances. Their latest show, 'Shooting From B...

item.title: President Ruto reassures Kenyans of stable fuel and fertiliser supply despite Middle East conflict
item.description: President William Ruto has reassured Kenyans that the country has enough fertiliser and fuel supplies despite disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Speaking after a high-level...

item.title: Education Initiatives Transform Johannesburg's Western CBD into a Tech and Learning Hub
item.description: Johannesburg's western CBD, once the bustling heart of the city's financial and mining sectors, has been facing decades of urban decay as businesses relocated north. Many of the historic office buildi...

item.title: Mental Fatigue Linked to Intensive AI Use in Work Environments
item.description: As artificial intelligence tools become more integrated into workplaces, some users are experiencing a phenomenon described by consultants at Boston Consulting Group as 'AI brain fry.' This state of m...

item.title: PCCB reports irregularities in nearly half of public projects monitored
item.description: The Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) has disclosed that out of 1,864 public projects monitored, 913 showed some form of irregularities. This was revealed by Director General Crispi...

item.title: PNP Confiscates Over P2 Billion in Smuggled Cigarettes Nationwide in Early 2026
item.description: From January 1 to March 29, 2026, the Philippine National Police (PNP) conducted a total of 3,153 anti-smuggling operations across the country, resulting in the confiscation of smuggled cigarettes val...

item.title: U.S. Army Reviews Video of Apache Helicopter Near Kid Rock’s Residence
item.description: The U.S. Army has launched an investigation into a video showing an Apache attack helicopter flying low near singer Kid Rock’s Nashville home. The video, posted by Kid Rock on social media, shows the ...

item.title: PNP enforces discipline as 740 police face sanctions in first quarter of 2026
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced that 740 of its personnel were subjected to administrative sanctions between January 1 and March 25, 2026, as part of its effort to promote accountabilit...

item.title: UK Government addresses diesel shortages and rising energy costs amid Middle East crisis
item.description: The UK is facing a serious energy challenge as the conflict in Iran disrupts the global oil supply, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer convened an emergency rou...

item.title: Bluesky Unveils Attie, an AI Tool That Lets Users Create Personalized Information Feeds
item.description: The team behind social media platform Bluesky has announced that it is developing a new standalone application called Attie. Presented during the Atmosphere conference in Canada, Attie is designed as ...

item.title: DSWD gives ₱5,000 cash aid each to over 256,000 Metro Manila public transport drivers to ease fuel cost burden
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) announced that it has disbursed ₱1.28 billion in cash assistance to more than 256,000 public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers across Metro Manila. ...

item.title: Sam Thaiday reflects on Gehamat Shibasaki’s return against Dolphins
item.description: Broncos legend Sam Thaiday has shared insights into his discussions with teammate Gehamat Shibasaki ahead of Shibasaki’s return to the field in the match against the Dolphins. Thaiday described his fe...

item.title: Amit Shah updates on talks with last active top Maoist, criticises Congress' historical ties
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Monday stated that negotiations are currently ongoing with the only remaining active member of the CPI (Maoist) central committee, presumed to be Mihir Besra in Jharkh...

item.title: Constitutional limits and the question of mental fitness in political leadership
item.description: Gaby Hinsliff’s opinion piece in *The Guardian* explores whether constitutional safeguards in the United States are strong enough to manage a situation in which a president shows signs of cognitive de...

item.title: Mandatory Cyber Security Oversight Needed for All Health Data Handlers in New Zealand
item.description: Following a spate of serious cyber security breaches affecting health data in New Zealand, the government has outlined a national cyber security strategy covering critical infrastructure, including he...

item.title: CAN President Urges Christians to Promote Peace and Unity Amid Challenges
item.description: As Nigerians dey face wahala with economy and wahala wey dey globally, Archbishop Daniel Okoh, wey be President of the Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), don call on Christians to maintain calm, ...

item.title: Tanzania expands road and bridge network with 79 major projects worth 6 trillion shillings
item.description: Dodoma: Tanzania Roads Agency (TANROADS) is pushing ahead with a major infrastructure push, currently executing 79 big road and bridge projects across the country valued at 6 trillion Tanzanian shilli...

item.title: Northern Governors Promise Support for Tinubu on Security and Economic Reforms
item.description: On Monday, the governors of the 19 northern states under the Northern States Governors’ Forum (NSGF) show clear support for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. Dem meet for Gombe State Governor’s Lodge, Asok...

item.title: Wallabies to face Ireland in Canberra for first time in a decade ahead of 2027 Rugby World Cup
item.description: The Wallabies are set to return to Canberra Stadium for the first time in ten years, taking on world number three Ireland in a major warm-up clash ahead of the 2027 Rugby World Cup. The match, locked ...
